content.problemStatement: You are given a list of integers $heights$ representing the heights of $N$ mountain peaks. For each peak, you need to find the height of the next higher peak that comes after it in the list. If there is no higher peak after a given peak, return $-1$ for that peak.
content.inputFormat: - The first line contains one integer $n$, the size of the list of integers.                                                                                                               - Next line contains $n$ integers $heights[0],heights[1]...heights[n]$, representing the peaks .
content.outputFormat: - For each peak, print the next higher peak that comes after it in the list. If there is no higher peak for a peak than print $-1$.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The next greater element for 1 is 2 and for 2 there is no next greater element so for 2 it will be -1.

For the first 6 there is no element greater than 6 on its right so the peak for the first 6 is -1. For 5 , 6 is the next peak as it is on the right side and is greater than 5. For the next element 3 , 6 is the peak as it is on the right side and is greater than 3, for the last 6 , no peak is there on the right side so -1 is there.
